title: festuca rubra subsp. arctica
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. arctica, also known as the arctic fescue or northern fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to the Arctic regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. arctica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Festuca rubra subsp. arctica can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
